analyst_observations: With-skill runs scored 8/8 on both evals; baseline runs scored 3/8 on both. The skill's deterministic generator script is what guarantees the exact 6pt/10pt spacer paragraphs, gray-shaded rate tables, divider style, and single trailing contact line - baselines invented their own (visually unrelated) layouts with comparison tables and recommendation sections.
Baseline runs were also slower and costlier: 106-143s and up to 35.6k tokens vs 61-65s and ~23.5k tokens with the skill, since baselines designed a document from scratch.

Non-discriminating assertion: 'Output is a valid .docx' passes in every configuration - both conditions reliably produce openable files. The discriminating assertions are all formatting-contract checks.

Edge cases worked: the 64-character room type stayed within page width (room column capped), and the unverified TripAdvisor hotel rendered 'TripAdvisor rating not verified' and was surfaced as a Needs Verification item.
Only 1 run per configuration this iteration, so no variance data yet; the generator is deterministic by construction, which is the property the user cares about.
